description: GBIV.md support - parse a GBIV.md file in the repo root and surface its features in gbiv status

userStories.title: Create gbiv_md parser module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a parser module that reads GBIV.md and returns a list of features so that the status command can display them.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/gbiv_md.rs with a GbivFeature struct: tag: Option<String>, description: String, notes: Vec<String>
Implement pub fn parse_gbiv_md(path: &std::path::Path) -> Vec<GbivFeature>
Lines starting with '- ' are treated as feature lines; optional [tag] prefix is extracted into the tag field
Subsequent lines that do not start with '- ' and are not '---' are stored as notes on the preceding feature
When a line '---' is encountered, parsing stops and remaining content is ignored
Returns empty vec when the file does not exist or contains no feature lines
Typecheck passes
Tests exist that verify the functionality of parsing
Tests pass

userStories.title: Register module and display GBIV.md features in gbiv status
userStories.description: As a user, I want gbiv status to show features from GBIV.md below the worktree list so that I can see my backlog at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Add mod gbiv_md; to src/main.rs

GBIV.md is always read from the main worktree's repository (gbiv_root/main/<repo>/GBIV.md), never from gbiv_root directly or from the current working directory; use find_repo_in_worktree(&gbiv_root.join("main")) to locate the repo path
In status_command, call parse_gbiv_md with the main worktree repo path joined with "GBIV.md" after collecting worktree results
If the feature list is non-empty, print a blank line followed by a dim 'GBIV.md' header line
For each feature, print the tag in its matching ANSI color (using ansi_color from colors.rs) or a dim 'backlog' label if untagged, followed by the feature description
When GBIV.md is absent or has no features, status output is unchanged
Typecheck passes
Tests exist that verify the functionality of gbiv status parsing the GBIV.md file
Tests pass

userStories.title: Document GBIV.md support in README
userStories.description: As a user, I want to understand the GBIV.md file format and how it appears in gbiv status so that I can start using it.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a 'GBIV.md' section to README.md under the Usage heading
Explain the file format: features start with '- ', optional [color] tag, notes on sub-lines, '---' stops parsing
Show an example GBIV.md file with tagged items, an untagged backlog item, and a note
Show example gbiv status output demonstrating the GBIV.md section beneath the worktree rows
Note that the file is optional and status output is unchanged when it is absent

userStories.title: Create GBIV.md template on gbiv init
userStories.description: As a user, I want gbiv init to create a starter GBIV.md file in my repo so that I can immediately start adding features to my backlog.
acceptanceCriteria: After successfully creating all worktrees, write a GBIV.md file to the main repo path (main_repo_path joined with 'GBIV.md')
The file content is exactly: 10 newline characters, then '---\n', then the usage explanation block

The usage explanation block reads: '# GBIV.md

Add features above the `---` line. Each feature starts with `- ` and an optional `[color]` tag.

Example:

- [red] My urgent feature
  A note about this feature
- [green] A less urgent feature
- An untagged backlog item

Supported tags match ROYGBIV colors: red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, violet.
Untagged items appear with a dim `backlog` label.
Everything below `---` is ignored by gbiv.'
If the file already exists, do not overwrite it
A success message 'Created GBIV.md' is printed after writing the file
Typecheck passes
Tests exist that verify GBIV.md is created with the correct content after init
Tests pass
